---
title: "Lab 8 – H-Bridge with Manual Switches"
canvas:
  type: assignment
  published: false
  points: 1
  grading_type: pass_fail
  submission_types: [online_upload]
  allowed_extensions: [pdf]
---

## Learning Goals

- Understand the H-bridge topology for bidirectional motor control
- Experience forward, reverse, coast, and brake modes
- Learn why shoot-through is destructive and must be prevented

## Background

Read the [H-Bridge Theory](01_HBridge_Theory.qmd) page before starting this lab.

In this lab you replace the MOSFETs with manual push-button switches to build intuition for how the H-bridge works. This is a conceptual exercise before you build the electronic version in Lab 9.

## Components

- 4× Tactile push-button switches
- 1× Small DC motor (or LED to observe current direction)
- 12V power supply **with current limit set to 500mA**
- Breadboard and jumper wires

::: {.callout-warning}
**Set the power supply current limit to 500mA** before starting. This protects against accidental shoot-through.
:::

## Tasks

1. Build the H-bridge using four push-button switches and a small motor.
2. Press S1 + S4 simultaneously. Which direction does the motor spin? (**Forward**)
3. Press S3 + S2 simultaneously. Does the motor reverse? (**Reverse**)
4. Release all switches while the motor is spinning. Does it stop immediately? (**Coast**)
5. While the motor is spinning, press S2 + S4 (both low-side switches). Does it stop faster? (**Brake**)
6. **Carefully** press S1 + S2 (same-leg switches, with current limit set!). What happens to the current reading on the power supply? **This is shoot-through.**

## Questions

1. Why is the H-bridge called an "H"-bridge? Draw the topology.
2. Explain the difference between coast mode and brake mode. When would you use each in the RC car?
3. What could go wrong if there is no dead-time when switching from forward to reverse electronically?
4. The BTS7960 motor driver has built-in shoot-through protection. Why is this important?

## Submission

Write a short lab report in Quarto following the [Report Writing Guide](../01_Fundamentals/01_Report_Writing_Guide.qmd). Include a diagram of your H-bridge circuit, describe what you observed in each mode (forward, reverse, coast, brake, shoot-through), and answer the questions. Render to PDF and upload.
